title: Printed circuit board and manufacturing method thereof
abstract: Provided is a printed circuit board using thermally and electrically conductive layer, and a manufacturing method thereof. The manufacturing method for mounting a plurality of elements includes forming an electrode layer on a substrate of a PCB, forming a photo solder resist (PSR) layer in a patterned manner on a first area of the electrode layer; forming a conductive layer on the PSR layer in the patterned manner, the conductive layer being configured to conduct heat and static electricity; and mounting a plurality of elements on a second area of the side of the PCB, the second area being different from the first area.
claims: Claims ( 15 ) A method of manufacturing a printed circuit board (PCB), the method comprising: arranging a substrate; forming an electrode layer on the substrate; forming a photo solder resist (PSR) layer in a patterned manner on a first area of a side of the electrode layer; forming a conductive layer on the PSR layer in the patterned manner, the conductive layer being configured to conduct heat and static electricity; and mounting a plurality of elements on a second area of the side of the PCB, the second area being different from the first area. The method of claim 1, wherein the conductive layer comprises graphene material. The method of claim 1, further comprising: forming a via hole which penetrates the PSR layer to connect the conductive layer and the electrode layer. The method of claim 1, wherein a height of the conductive layer from the surface of the PCB is greater than a height of one or more of the plurality of elements from the surface of the PCB. The method of claim 1, wherein the substrate comprises a  flexible material, and wherein the plurality of elements comprise at least one micro light emitting diode. The method of claim 1, further comprises: connecting the conductive layer with a ground layer of the PCB. The method of claim 1, wherein the conductive layer is formed using a silk screen method. The method of claim 1, wherein the conductive layer is formed an ink jet printing method. The method of claim 1, wherein the conductive layer is formed using a lithography method. The method of claim 1, further comprising: forming an additional conductive layer for conducting heat and static electricity on a third area different the first area and the second area. A printed circuit board (PCB) comprising: a substrate for mounting a plurality of elements; an electrode layer formed on a substrate; a photo solder resist (PSR) layer formed in a patterned manner on a first area of a side of the electrode layer; and a conductive layer formed on the PSR layer in the patterned manner, the conductive layer being configured to conduct heat and static electricity. The PCB of claim 11, wherein the conductive layer comprises graphene material. The PCB of claim 11, further comprising: a via hole which penetrates the PSR layer to connect the conductive layer and the electrode layer. The PCB of claim 11, wherein a height of the conductive layer from the surface of the PCB is greater than a height of one or more of the plurality of elements from the surface of the PCB. The PCB of claim 11, wherein the substrate comprises a flexible material, and wherein the plurality of elements comprise at least one micro light emitting diode (LED).
